commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=891061dffadf28d4bfdff080ef8b6542aeb6ac5c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a default-off Web UI sidebar All agents toggle, local state/CSS, request-local unscoped sessions.list loading, and regression tests for showing spawned subagent rows while preserving explicit scoped loads.

PR surface: Source +54, Tests +106. Total +160 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main scopes UI sessions.list requests by agent and the sidebar filters out subagent/spawned rows. I did not run a live browser/gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real browser/gateway proof, a refresh-consistency repair, and maintainer selection among sibling fix shapes before merge.

target.title: fix #95295: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95295
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The Web UI sidebar Recent Sessions list could hide existing child-spawned subagent transcripts when the current chat scope was another agent, so users could not intentionally rediscover those sessions from the left sidebar.
- Solution: Keep the existing scoped/default behavior, but add an explicit sidebar **All agents** mode that uses the existing unscoped `sessions.list`/combined-store path and allows subagent/spawned rows in that opt-in view.
- What changed: Added sidebar state and UI for the scope toggle, made only the sidebar toggle request omit scoped `agentId`, kept cron/global/unknown/archived rows excluded, and added controller/rendering regression tests.
- What did NOT change: This does not change the chat picker, default sidebar scoping, shared `loadSessions` explicit `agentId` semantics, gateway protocol schema, stored transcript format, migration behavior, or default visibility of subagent/spawned sessions.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users with persisted child-spawned subagent work can intentionally find and reopen those existing transcripts from the sidebar without losing the safer scoped default view.
- Fixes #95295

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sidebar Recent Sessions can now opt in to cross-agent discovery so an existing row such as `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` with `spawnedBy` can appear when the user enables **All agents**, while default scoped mode still hides those rows.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repository worktree on Windows/Git Bash with Node v22.17.0 and pnpm-installed workspace dependencies. The repo engine warning requested Node `>=22.19.0`, but the focused Vitest command completed successfully.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; checked `command -v sonarlint || command -v sonar-scanner || command -v sonar-scanner-cli`.
- **Evidence after fix:** The focused Vitest command exited 0 and covered the sidebar renderer plus session controller path. The diff whitespace check exited 0. The scanner availability command found no SonarLint/sonar-scanner executable in PATH.
- **Observed result after fix:** The controller regression verifies explicit non-sidebar scoped refreshes still send `agentId`; the renderer regressions verify the All agents control remains reachable with no recent rows and that a cross-agent spawned subagent row is displayed ahead of the main row while a cron row remains excluded.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a browser/manual UI session or a live gateway process with real persisted transcripts, and I could not run SonarLint because no scanner executable was available in PATH.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Disclosed proof gap — redacted real browser/gateway proof is still unavailable in this environment; local regression proof is listed in Real behavior proof and Regression Test Plan.
- **RF-002:** Disclosed proof gap — live persisted-transcript UI proof was not available; the PR body explicitly states the unavailable browser/gateway proof and the completed focused validation.
- **RF-003:** Disclosed proof gap — persisted spawned-transcript proof in default scoped mode and opt-in All agents mode still requires a maintainer/Mantis-capable environment.
- **RF-004:** Maintainer decision needed — sibling candidate PRs exist; this PR intentionally keeps the fix UI-only and same-contract rather than adding protocol/API surface.
- **RF-005:** Maintainer decision needed — the default-safe UX is explicit and default-off, but maintainer confirmation is still appropriate because the opt-in view expands visible session rows across agent scopes.
- **RF-006:** Partially addressed / maintainer review needed — session-state defects called out in the review findings were fixed below, while live proof and canonical product-direction choice remain maintainer-gated.
- **RF-007:** Fixed — kept the all-agents override out of shared loads by restoring `loadSessions` so explicit `agentId` overrides remain authoritative; only the sidebar toggle request omits `agentId` when entering all-agents mode. Added a controller regression for explicit scoped refreshes.
- **RF-008:** Fixed — the sidebar Recent Sessions section now keeps the **All agents** scope control reachable whenever the sidebar is expanded, even if the scoped recent list is empty. Added a renderer regression for the empty-list case.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, with neighboring focused suites `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` run for sidebar/chat rendering coverage.
- **Scenario locked in:** Explicit non-sidebar `loadSessions(..., { agentId })` requests keep their scoped `agentId`; the sidebar All agents control is reachable with an empty recent list; and a cross-agent `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` row with `spawnedBy` renders in Recent Sessions all-agents mode while cron rows remain filtered.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The controller test guards the request boundary so shared agent-scoped refreshes cannot be accidentally widened, and the render tests guard the sidebar reveal/reachability filters without expanding into unrelated gateway/protocol tests.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state / security-boundary / compatibility.
- **Risk explanation:** The diff touches session-state UI filtering and a scope control, which can affect whether internal child-spawned transcripts are visible. Compatibility risk is intentionally limited because explicit `loadSessions` agent overrides remain authoritative and no public protocol, schema, config, storage format, or migration contract changes. Related open PR scan found broader approaches for this issue, including PRs that added protocol/API surface; this PR intentionally chooses a narrower same-contract UI path instead.
- **Why acceptable:** The new behavior is default-off, explicitly user-selected, and limited to the sidebar Recent Sessions list. Existing exclusions for archived/global/unknown/cron rows remain. Default scoped behavior, chat picker behavior, and shared scoped refresh behavior are not changed, so the security-boundary/session-state risk is bounded and reviewable.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root cause was duplicated scoping at the sidebar boundary: the sidebar request used the current agent's `agentId`, and the sidebar Recent Sessions filter then rejected subagent-keyed or `spawnedBy` rows. Those source filters prevented already-persisted child-spawned sessions from reaching the sidebar view across agent scopes.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix changes only the sidebar-owned filters instead of masking missing rows downstream: opt-in all-agents mode makes the sidebar toggle request use the existing unscoped `sessions.list` path so the combined session store can return cross-agent rows, and the sidebar filter admits subagent/spawned rows only in that explicit mode while preserving cron/global/unknown/archive guards.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings for added `return false` guard branches are expected filter predicates, not symptom-masking defaults: they preserve existing exclusions for cron/global/unknown/archived sessions and the default-off subagent/spawned hiding behavior. The public-contract warning is addressed by keeping the canonical protocol/schema unchanged and using the existing unscoped `sessions.list` behavior only from the sidebar toggle request.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary remains the existing `sessions.list` contract and combined session store; this patch does not add schema/protocol/config/data-format changes. The canonical behavior change is confined to the Web UI sidebar state/render/request boundary, with related open PRs considered and broader protocol changes left out of scope.
target.author: mikasa0818

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
